title: D+Q Senolytic Combo Triggers Brain Myelin Damage in PNAS Study -- Raises Caution for Prophylactic Use
description: A landmark PNAS study from UConn's Crocker lab reveals that dasatinib plus quercetin (D+Q), the most widely used senolytic combination, causes profound myelin loss in the brain of treated mice, with worse damage in younger animals. The corpus callosum disappeared in treated mice, and oligodendrocytes reverted to an immature, non-functional state. This warrants significant caution for prophylactic off-label D+Q use, while opening new insights into MS-like demyelination.

title: APOE4 Accelerates Brain Aging Decades Before Alzheimer's Symptoms -- Nell2 Gene Identified as Rescue Target
description: Published in Nature Aging, Gladstone Institute researchers show that APOE4 triggers neuronal hyperexcitability in the hippocampus of young mice through increased Nell2 protein production, mimicking a process that normally occurs only with aging. CRISPR knockdown of Nell2 fully rescued the dysfunction. Since APOE4 is carried by roughly 1 in 4 people, this identifies a druggable early intervention target for Alzheimer's prevention.

title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Draws Millions in Premiere Weekend -- Longevity Goes Mainstream
description: CNN's six-part longevity docuseries premiered Saturday April 11, featuring interviews with Sam Altman, Bryan Johnson, Jennifer Doudna, and Scott Galloway. The show examines biotech labs, South Korea's healthcare system, and the difference between evidence-based longevity science and tech-bro hype, bringing mainstream attention to the field at a critical moment.

title: AI-Powered Senolytic Discovery: USPTO Patent Covers 800,000+ Screened Molecules Targeting Bcl-2
description: The USPTO published patent application US20260097022A1 on April 9, covering AI-trained neural networks that screened 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and predicted activity across 800,000+ molecules. The disclosed compounds selectively target senescent cells via Bcl-2 binding with favorable medicinal chemistry properties. This represents a major scale-up in computational senolytic drug discovery.

title: Plant-Based Diets Slow Epigenetic Aging Across Multiple Clocks in 4,866-Person Study
description: Published in Aging-US and selected as Editors' Choice, a study analyzing ARIC and NHANES data (n=4,866) found that higher adherence to plant-based diets consistently decelerated GrimAge2, PhenoAge, and HannumAge clocks. Healthy plant-based patterns showed the strongest effects; unhealthy plant-based patterns showed no benefit. Practical evidence for longevity enthusiasts optimizing diet.

title: D+Q Senolytic Combo Causes Brain Myelin Damage in Mice (PNAS)
description: UConn researchers found dasatinib+quercetin causes profound myelin loss in mouse brains, worse in younger animals. The corpus callosum disappeared in treated mice, and oligodendrocytes reverted to a dysfunctional immature state.

title: APOE4 Triggers Early Hippocampal Hyperexcitability via Nell2 Protein (Nature Aging)
description: APOE4 increases Nell2 production in hippocampal neurons, causing shrinkage and hyperexcitability years before cognitive decline. CRISPR knockdown of Nell2 rescues the dysfunction, identifying a druggable target for Alzheimer's prevention.

title: CNN Premieres 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Six-Part Longevity Docuseries
description: Six-part series follows Kara Swisher investigating longevity science, featuring Sam Altman, Bryan Johnson, Jennifer Doudna, and visits to biotech labs and South Korea's healthcare system.

title: USPTO Publishes AI Senolytic Compound Patent: Neural Networks Screen 800,000+ Molecules
description: Patent US20260097022A1 covers screening 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and AI neural networks predicting activity across 800,000+ molecules, targeting Bcl-2 proteins in senescent cells.

title: Plant-Based Dietary Patterns Linked to Slower Epigenetic Aging (Editors' Choice)
description: Analysis of ARIC and NHANES data (n=4,866) found plant-rich diets decelerate GrimAge2, PhenoAge, and HannumAge clocks. Healthy plant-based patterns showed strongest effects; unhealthy plant-based diets showed no benefit.

title: Psilocybin and Human Longevity: npj Aging Study Tempers Media Hype
description: Despite mouse data showing psilocybin extends lifespan, an analysis of prominent psychedelic advocates' mortality found they did not outlive cancer and aging researchers. Highlights the need for rigorous human studies before inferring anti-aging effects.

title: Meal Timing Impacts Biological Aging Across Multiple Organs
description: Study finds eating the last meal between 3-5pm slows whole-body and heart aging versus after 9pm. Extended feeding windows over 16 hours are associated with faster aging, with effects varying by organ, sex, and diet quality.

title: NMN Plus Apigenin Restores Muscle, Bone, and Gut Health in Aged Mice via SIRT3
description: Combining NMN (NAD+ precursor) with apigenin (CD38 inhibitor) restores NAD+ levels, reduces senescence markers, and improves muscle strength, bone density, and gut microbiome diversity in aged mice.

title: Somatostatin Converts Brain Microglia to Protective Mode, Clears Amyloid in Alzheimer's Mice
description: DGIST researchers showed somatostatin directly regulates microglia to suppress neuroinflammation, reduce amyloid beta, and improve spatial memory in Alzheimer's mice. Approved drugs targeting SST receptors already exist.

title: Nature Feature: Partial Cellular Reprogramming Enters First Human Trial
description: Nature published an in-depth feature on Life Biosciences' ER-100, the first partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy entering human trials for glaucoma and NAION using three Yamanaka factors.

title: Life Biosciences' FDA-Cleared Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation?
description: Analysis of how Life Biosciences' ER-100 IND clearance signals the FDA is establishing a measured playbook for longevity developers, focused on specific diseases with accepted clinical outcomes.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin (April 8-9) reframed aging as progressive loss of coordination between biological systems rather than a single defect to fix.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Phase 1 RLS-1496: First GPX4 Modulator Shows Senolytic Efficacy in Humans
description: Rubedo's first-in-class GPX4 modulator RLS-1496 met its primary endpoint in Phase 1, with dose-dependent senescent cell reduction and improvement in psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Trial
description: NIA-funded study will test rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin in over 700 older adults to determine optimal dosing and whether these drugs slow biological aging.

title: Senosensitizers: New Drug Class Could Unlock Senolytic-Resistant Zombie Cells
description: Cedars-Sinai preclinical study finds senolytics clear 30-70% of senescent cells, but resistant cells remain. A new drug class called senosensitizers could enable elimination of these remaining ticking time bombs.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Q1 2026: $3.74B Raised Across 49 Deals
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events raising $3.74 billion, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with a projected full-year range of $8-9 billion in longevity biotech investment.

title: Eli Lilly Signs $2.75B Deal with Insilico Medicine for AI-Developed Oral Therapeutics
description: Eli Lilly expanded its AI drug development partnership with Insilico Medicine in a deal worth up to $2.75 billion for exclusive worldwide licensing of preclinical oral therapeutics.

title: Fight Aging: Remaining Challenges in Partial Reprogramming Therapies
description: Fight Aging reviews the key hurdles facing partial epigenetic reprogramming as Life Biosciences' first trial commences, including controlled delivery, dosing precision, and scaling beyond the eye.

title: Influenza Vaccination Attenuates Heart Attack and Stroke Risk After Flu Infection
description: Danish registry study covering 2014-2025 found PCR-confirmed influenza sharply increased AMI/stroke risk, but prior vaccination cut the excess risk roughly in half (IRR 4.7 unvaccinated vs 2.4 vaccinated).

title: King Penguin Study: Sedentary Lifestyle Accelerates Epigenetic Aging by 2.5-6.5 Years
description: Zoo-housed king penguins show accelerated epigenetic aging comparable to smoking effects in humans, yet still live longer. Suggests Western lifestyles extend lifespan but compress healthspan.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Refined Diet, Rapamycin Dropped
description: Blueprint 2026 refines macros to 2,250 cal with 130g protein, eliminates rapamycin due to side effects, and emphasizes measurement-first supplement decisions based on lab results.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Comprehensive LinkedIn analysis of longevity biotech investment strategy for family offices. The market is valued at $30.7B in 2026, projected to reach $52.8B by 2035, with key areas including epigenetic reprogramming and senolytics.

title: 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology: Dubai April 20-21
description: The 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology takes place April 20-21 in Dubai, UAE, with sessions on geriatric medicine, healthy aging, and longevity interventions.

title: Rapamycin Review: Comprehensive Analysis of Pros, Cons, and Evidence Gaps
source: Center for Food as Medicine & Longevity
description: In-depth analysis covering the PEARL trial, PhenoAge modeling, and comprehensive literature review concludes: rapamycin is safe at low doses for ~1 year, but zero qualifying sources directly demonstrate slowed biological aging in healthy humans.
